summary: The Docker-based reproduction demonstrates that an authenticated user with SOAP rights can set the pictureUri field to a path traversal string via the REST API and then read the traversed file content through the profile image endpoint, because isRelative() does not block ../ sequences and OmFileHelper.getUserProfilePicture() passes the unsanitized uri directly into File construction.
expected_security_impact: The profile image endpoint at /profile/<userId> should return the contents of /etc/passwd (or another target file) instead of a profile picture, demonstrating that arbitrary files can be read from the server filesystem.
actual_observed_result: Reproduction was not attempted in this run. The vulnerability was confirmed through static code analysis tracing the data flow from pictureUri setting through REST API to file serving via ProfileImageResourceReference.
verification_method: The verification script checks whether the response from /profile/<userId> contains content from /etc/passwd (specifically looking for the 'root:' line typical of passwd files). A successful traversal is distinguished from a normal profile image response by checking for file content signatures versus PNG image headers.
evidence_produced_by_run: Static analysis confirmed ProfileImageResourceReference.java:72-80 isRelative() only checks URI.isAbsolute() and does not block ../ sequences
Static analysis confirmed OmFileHelper.java:181 constructs new File(getUploadProfilesUserDir(userId), uri) without traversal validation
Static analysis confirmed UserMapper.java:61 copies dto.getPictureUri() to User entity without sanitization
Static analysis confirmed ProfileImageResourceReference.java:112-114 reads the traversed file via FileInputStream and returns its contents
limitations_or_blockers: Building Apache OpenMeetings from source requires Maven and significant build time; the full build was not executed during this run
The reproduction requires a running OpenMeetings server with database initialization, SOAP-enabled user, and REST API access

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit or ref, and mounts that checkout into the container.
